final_text:
I'll start by loading the triage tool schemas and then query for the relevant suppliers.Now let me query for US-owned suppliers, sorted by risk score ascending, to identify candidates for clearing.I've identified the qualifying suppliers: US-owned with a risk score **strictly under 30**. That's 9 suppliers (scores 12–29). S-012 at exactly 30 is excluded. All 9 are currently unreviewed. I'll submit clear decisions for all of them in parallel now.All 9 suppliers have been cleared. Here's a summary of what was done:

| ID | Supplier | Program | Risk Score | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| S-008 | Cobalt Fabrication Corp | GMLRS | 12 | ✅ Cleared |
| S-009 | Pioneer Aerospace LLC | B-21 | 18 | ✅ Cleared |
| S-010 | Sierra Propulsion Holdings | Columbia-class | 22 | ✅ Cleared |
| S-035 | Monarch Microelectronics Holdings | F-35 | 22 | ✅ Cleared |
| S-020 | Summit Precision Corp | GMLRS | 23 | ✅ Cleared |
| S-026 | Vanguard Systems Holdings | GMLRS | 24 | ✅ Cleared |
| S-025 | Highland Precision Co | F-35 | 25 | ✅ Cleared |
| S-011 | Cascade Composites Co | F-35 | 28 | ✅ Cleared |
| S-017 | Aegis Systems Corp | Columbia-class | 29 | ✅ Cleared |

**Boundary note:** S-012 (Redstone Microelectronics Inc, score 30) was deliberately excluded — "under 30" is a strict bound, and it sits exactly at 30.
